title: Talisman (artifact)
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Talisman}} {{Conflicting|''[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee]]''|''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''}} {{Artifact |image=[[File:Thetalisman.jpg]] |name=Talisman |manufacturer= |model= |type= |origin= |culture= |creators= |created= |destroyed=Between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] and [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' places the [[Mission to Panna Prime|mission]] to [[Panna Prime]] after the [[Evacuation of Yavin]], which itself was placed six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], corresponding to [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. {{SW|url=news/the-best-holidays-and-celebrations-in-the-galaxy-everybodys-invited-of-course|text=The Best Holidays and Celebrations in the Galaxy…and Empire Day, Too|int=The Best Holidays and Celebrations in the Galaxy…and Empire Day, Too|nolive=1}} states that the events portrayed in ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' take place one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the Battle of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin]], corresponding to [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. Therefore, the events of the mission as portrayed in ''[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee]]'', which included the discovery and destruction of the talisman, must have occurred between 0.5 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]] and 1 ABY. |discovered=Between 0.5 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]] and 1 ABY |owners= |locations=[[Panna Prime]]''[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee]]'' |cost= |value= |shape= |size= |color=[[Color/Legends|Gray]] |material= |length= |width= |height= |weight= |protection= |capacity= |cont= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose=Spread the [[sleeping virus]] |heritage= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]{{SWArchive|url=episode-v/bts/article/f20070124/index.html|text=Time Capsule: Darth Vader 1980}} }} {{Quote|Starlog 3-24-1 from the bridge of the cruiser ''RS Revenge'', Captain Kazan reporting. We are awaiting the return of Captain Han Solo and his first mate, Chewbacca, who are long overdue on a delicate mission to acquire the mystical talisman which has been sought by our forces and by the Empire.|Captain Kazan|}} The '''talisman''' was a false artifact used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in a plot to bait the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] with rumors of its invisibility-granting qualities. In actuality, it was infected with a [[sleeping virus]] that only affected [[Human/Legends|humans]]. Sometime between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] and [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], the talisman was found by Rebel operatives [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Solo was affected by the virus and was tied upside down by Chewbacca to keep him alive by ensuring [[Blood/Legends|blood]] flow to his head. Chewbacca destroyed the talisman in a trash chute aboard the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], though not before fellow Rebel [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] was affected. Skywalker and Solo were both saved thanks to a [[Serum (sleeping virus)|serum]] acquired by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who was on a mission from the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to find the [[Ring Base|Rebel base]]. ==Description== {{Quote|The talisman. It makes things invisible, I think.''"
[squeals]
"''Yes even you. Which I think might be an improvement.|C-3PO and R2-D2|}} The talisman was a false artifact that was [[Color/Legends|gray]] in appearance and seemed to glitter. It had a round base with the protruding shape of an [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] creature sticking out from it. Some [[Writing/Legends|writing]] was inscribed on the talisman below the insectoid. It also had a purple ribbon attached to it. The talisman was believed to have the power to make things invisible, but it actually spread a [[sleeping virus]] engineered by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] that only affected [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. ==History== {{Quote|R2-D2 has detected contamination, it must have come from the talisman.|C-3PO|}} The false talisman was infected with the sleeping virus by the Empire at the behest of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who hoped to find the location of various bases associated with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], with whom the Empire was at [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]]. Various reports were then spread about the talisman's invisibility powers, attracting the attention of the Alliance, who also believed that the Empire sought the talisman. Between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] and [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Vader tasked the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] with finding the bases, and therefore Fett allowed{{DB|character|bobafett|Fett, Boba}} Rebel agents [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] to recover the talisman. The Human Solo was affected by the sleeping virus and hung upside down by the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] Chewbacca inside their [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], to allow [[Blood/Legends|blood]] to flow to his head and prevent his [[Death/Legends|death]]. Chewbacca steered the ''Millennium Falcon'' nearly to a [[Ring Base|base]] past its expected arrival [[Time/Legends|time]], and eventually crashed the vessel on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Panna Prime]]. Solo's friend [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] left to [[Mission to Panna Prime|rescue]] them along with the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. [[File:SleepingVirus.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Han Solo (left) and Luke Skywalker tied up after exposure to the sleeping virus by the talisman.]] Fett encountered Skywalker on the moon and helped him locate the ''Millennium Falcon'', where they walked in on Chewbacca depositing the talisman in a trash chute.[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee (Little Golden Book)|''The Story of the Faithful Wookiee'' Little Golden Book]] While calling out for the Wookiee to stop, Skywalker became affected by the sleeping virus. Chewbacca explained the situation—in his native [[Shyriiwook/Legends|Shyriiwook]] [[Language/Legends|language]]—to the droids, who [[Translator/Legends|translated]] for Fett. Together, the droids concluded that the virus originated from the talisman. After they tied Skywalker up next to Solo, Fett and Chewbacca then traveled to [[Panna City]] to find an [[Serum (sleeping virus)|antidote]], as Vader had planned. The Imperial plot was foiled when C-3PO and R2-D2 intercepted a transmission between Vader and Fett. The incident was later portrayed in a [[Unidentified Rebel cartoon|cartoon]] narrated by the Alliance [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kazan]]. Records of the incident were later classified. ==Behind the scenes== The talisman first appeared in ''[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee]]'', a cartoon written by [[George Lucas]] and produced by [[Nelvana]] Studios.{{SWArchive|url=movies/saga/20081210.html|text=Tooning Out the "Holiday Special": Nelvana Studios}} It originally aired on television in [[1978]] as part of ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]''.''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' The [[1995]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' stated that part of Fett's motivation—as revealed in a transmission to Vader—was to acquire the talisman for himself and capture the Rebels.''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' This conflicted with the events portrayed in the cartoon, where Fett's mission was to find the Rebel base and no reference was made to him looking for the talisman. This article follows the cartoon's version of events. ==Appearances== *''[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee]]'' {{1st}} *[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee (Little Golden Book)|''The Story of the Faithful Wookiee'' Little Golden Book]] ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{InsiderCite|73|A State of Nelvana: Droids & Ewoks Adventure Hour Revisited|A State of Nelvana: ''Droids & Ewoks Adventure Hour'' Revisited|reprint=1}} *{{SWArchive|url=episode-v/bts/article/f20070124/index.html|text=Time Capsule: Darth Vader 1980}} *{{CSWECite|I||Fett, Boba}} *{{InsiderCite|106|Classic Moment|Classic Moment: Boba Fett Encounter}} *{{DB|character|bobafett|Fett, Boba}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:1st-century ABY archaeological discoveries]] [[Category:Amulets and talismans]]
